content:
First of all, IвАЩm a player whoвАЩs been playing every day since the game launched five days in. IвАЩve been through the nerf of the lucky bags, Mi LiвАЩs awful storyline, the addition of Overlimit and +12 Fate Wheels, the half-anniversary, various character/Fate Wheel buffs, all the Deep Dive revampsвАФIвАЩve gotten many #1 ranks there. IвАЩve also seen all the PvP revamps and gotten #1 several times, and all the Disaster Tolerance revamps where I got full scores many times. Then it became one new character every two months, plus the revival bracket nonsense, the terrible changes in the Astral Chapter, and both obvious and hidden nerfs to characters. Weak characters get laughably weak buffs that feel like internal hate, while strong characters get ridiculously OP buffs that feel like internal favoritism.

I used to have the motivation to fight for #1 in Deep Dive every day, but now just opening a match disgusts me. I used to have the urge to hop into PvP every time a patch droppedвАФsometimes multiple times a patchвАФbut now each version lasts 42 days with barely any changes, and the imbalance just makes me sick. IвАЩve been through my own PvP main getting brutally nerfed multiple times to the point I wanted to sell my account, but the pick-ban mode kept me around. Now even that mode is as trash as the premade decks, with turn order deciding way too many matches.

It used to be one new character every 21 days, now itвАЩs every 42 days. Back then you had no money and lost the revival bracket so no dev capacity, but now you have money and dev capacity and even won the revival bracketвАФso why does it take even longer than before to release a single character? Did your dev capacity turn into money for big-name voice actors and radio dramas? Or have you been focusing on the JP server, kowtowing to foreign players? WhereвАЩs the PvP content? ItвАЩs just tweaking a few numbers, and you canвАЩt even do that? Ever since you fully took over PvP, all IвАЩve felt is disgustвАФwhereвАЩs the fun?

content: You don't have to care about him. It's like a killer knocking on the door with a knife. His friends are saying don't open the door for him. He will hurt you, but he says: You can't say he will if it doesn't happen. I know he will hurt me, but I don't think he will hurt me. You have to provide data that he will hurt me, and I don't think I have to provide data that won't hurt me. There's no obligation. My obligation is to reject this statement, he said so, you believe him, there is no need to care.
